pattern: Any character that appears fewer than k times overall can never be inside a valid substring, so splitting on those characters and recursing on each piece finds the longest qualifying run.
hints: If a character appears fewer than k times in the whole string, it cannot belong to any valid substring, which tells you where to cut.
Split the string at every too-rare character and solve the same problem on each remaining piece, since a valid answer never crosses a forbidden character.
Recurse: count frequencies, find a character below k, split there and take the best sub-answer; if no such character exists the whole piece already qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: longer window with two qualifying letters
no character reaches k so answer is zero
rare middle character splits the string

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings with small k so splits and recursion fire
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randrange(1, 6)
    return [s, k]
stress.note: small alphabet plus small k produces many qualifying windows and splits
